description: Aggregated benchmark data from 25+ external TA tech, recruitment marketing, job board, analyst, and staffing reports (2024-2026). All data sourced from publicly available reports and press releases.

recruitics_talent_market_index_2025.title: Talent Market Index (Monthly Reports, 2025)
recruitics_talent_market_index_2025.methodology: Aggregates billions of data points from job advertisements and proprietary Recruitics data, tracking CPC and CPA across 9 job categories monthly

key_metrics.note: Reports percentage changes rather than absolute CPC dollar values

key_findings: Year-over-year talent attraction prices continue to surge in many categories, signaling increased competition for talent
Healthcare remains the highest-cost category for talent attraction
Seasonal patterns significantly impact month-over-month pricing, with sharp swings in hospitality and retail
Transportation & Logistics prices remain persistently elevated YoY despite monthly fluctuations
Finance & Ops and IT showed sharp surges mid-year, signaling return to growth in strategic functions

appcast_benchmark_2025.title: 2025 Recruitment Marketing Benchmark Report (9th Annual, covering 2024 data)
appcast_benchmark_2025.methodology: Analyzed 379 million job ad clicks and 30+ million applies from 1,300+ U.S. employers. Pay-for-performance recruitment ads only (CPC or CPA basis). Search/social section: 150+ employers, 1.7 billion impressions, 35 million clicks.

cpc_trend_2024.note: Rose over 27% in 2024, driven by harder-to-fill roles and job board pricing

cpa_trend_2024.note: Stable; down substantially from 2023 across all occupations

cph_2024.note: Appcast CPH reflects cost of recruitment advertising spend per hire, not total cost per hire

apply_rate_by_occupation.note: Healthcare, construction, skilled trades had lowest apply rates with highest CPAs

sitting_down_jobs.description: White-collar office roles (tech, marketing, consulting, HR)
sitting_down_jobs.apply_rate_trend: Higher apply rates due to increased competition from layoffs
sitting_down_jobs.cpa_trend: Lower CPAs due to candidate oversupply

standing_up_jobs.cpa_trend: Higher CPAs due to talent shortages

appcast_benchmark_2025.geographic_insight: Every state experienced lower CPAs in 2024 compared to prior year
key_findings: Labor market in near-perfect balance, shifting power to employers ('Great Stay' era)
CPC rose 27% but CPA only 4.8% due to rising apply rates offsetting cost increases
CPH (recruitment ad spend per hire) remained flat at ~$750, peaking at $851 year-end
Occupational divide: white-collar roles saw high apply rates while healthcare/trades struggled
Balanced recruitment strategy should incorporate search, display, and social media channels

appcast_benchmark_2026_preview.title: 2026 Recruitment Marketing Benchmark Report (10th Annual, covering 2025 data)

key_metrics.cpa_trend_2025: Rose sharply despite softer labor market
key_metrics.cph_trend_2025: Rose sharply, driven by shifts in job board pricing and programmatic media models
key_metrics.apply_rates_2025: Remained high, fueled by increased job-seeker volume
key_metrics.remote_work_trend: Remote jobs began losing footing after years of outperforming in-person roles

key_findings: CPA and CPH rose sharply in 2025 despite softer labor market
White-collar recession deepened with apply rates for office roles rising significantly
Frontline standing-up roles, especially healthcare, remained costly and hard to fill
Shifts in job board pricing and programmatic media models drove cost increases

wordstream_localiq_google_ads_2025.title: Google Ads Benchmarks 2025: Competitive Data & Insights for Every Industry
wordstream_localiq_google_ads_2025.methodology: Analyzed 16,000+ search campaigns across Google and Microsoft Ads, Apr 2024 - Mar 2025. Covers 23 industries. Largest publicly available dataset of Google Ads performance.
key_findings: Average CPC rose to $5.26, up 12.88% YoY; CPC increased for 87% of industries
Average CTR improved to 6.66%, up 3.74% YoY, indicating better ad relevance
Average CVR at 7.52%; 65% of industries saw improved conversion rates
Average CPL at $70.11; CPL increase only ~5% YoY (vs 25% prior year)
Smart bidding campaigns saw sharper CPC increases as Google controls those CPCs
Beauty & Personal Care saw largest CPC increase at 60.1% YoY
Legal Services CPC decreased slightly from $8.95 to $8.58 despite being most competitive
Performance Max adoption lowering CPC in visual-heavy industries like Real Estate

radancy_ta_insights_2025.title: Radancy Talent Acquisition Insights & Fosway AI Report 2025

applicant_to_interview_rate.note: Only 17% of applicants made it to interview stage (Josh Bersin data)

application_abandonment_rate.note: 60% abandoned applications that were too slow or complex

poor_candidate_communication.note: Less than 4 in 10 candidates received regular communication

offer_decline_due_to_poor_experience.note: More than 25% turned down a job due to poor experience

ai_full_maturity.note: Only 1% of companies have fully matured AI in hiring

key_findings: Average cost per hire approximately $4,700, making over-reliance on external hiring costly
AI leaders implementing AI at 3-5x the rate of average organizations
Organizations investing in AI already reducing costs, shortening time to hire, improving quality of hires
WEF estimates 39% of core skills will change by 2030; 63% of employers see skills gaps as top barrier
51% of employees actively looking for new roles in 2024, equalling highest level since 2014

pandologic_veritone_2025.title: Evolution of Programmatic Job Advertising: Strategic Talent Acquisition

key_metrics.data_points_in_platform: 5 billion+ job advertising performance data points
key_metrics.programmatic_growth_vs_market: 2x faster than overall job advertising market

key_findings: 56% of companies plan to increase programmatic job advertising investment in 2025
50% of companies still don't fully understand what programmatic advertising is
Only 34% of enterprise companies currently use programmatic job advertising
Quality of hire and cost-per-qualified-application becoming standard metrics
Pay-for-performance models quickly gaining market share from pay-for-duration

phenom_ai_automation_2026.title: State of AI & Automation for HR: 2026 Benchmarks Report
phenom_ai_automation_2026.methodology: Analyzed nearly 500 organizations using Phenom's proprietary AI & Automation Maturity Model (Level 0-5) across 12+ industries

low_ai_maturity_organizations.note: 83% scored within lowest two maturity categories

high_intelligence_maturity.note: Less than 1% reached high intelligence maturity

organizations_scaled_ai.note: Only 25% have successfully scaled AI initiatives

key_findings: 83% of organizations demonstrate low AI and automation maturity in HR
Less than 1% reached high intelligence maturity levels
Significant variation in AI adoption by sector and function
Key opportunity: automating hiring workflows to cut time-to-hire
AI matching can improve quality-of-hire, reduce bias, and increase retention

icims_workforce_report_2025.title: iCIMS Workforce Reports (Monthly, 2025)
icims_workforce_report_2025.methodology: Proprietary data from 3+ million global platform users, 200+ million applications, 5.5+ million hires annually

recruiters_expect_ai_in_roles.note: Nearly all say new hires will manage/work alongside AI agents in next 2 years

key_findings: Applicants per opening at series high of 34, creating buyer's market for employers
White-collar jobs draw nearly 2x average APO; healthcare remains critically short
Entry-level unemployment hit 6.6%, highest in a decade excluding pandemic
Applications up 21% but hiring down 8%, reflecting employer caution
96% of recruiters say new hires will manage or work alongside AI agents within 2 years

employ_recruiter_nation_2025.title: Recruiter Nation 2025: What's Shaping Talent Acquisition Today

top_priority_more_candidates.note: Up from 46%, surpassing candidate quality for first time in 2 years

key_findings: 70% of recruiters report increased hiring year-over-year
Getting more candidates per role (52%) surpassed candidate quality as top priority
Rising candidate expectations jumped from 24% to 42% as a key challenge
67% plan to increase tech spending, with AI tools as the top investment area
First-year turnover dropped 49% from 23.7% to 12.1% ('Great Stay' effect)

smartrecruiters_benchmarks_2025.title: Recruiting Benchmarks 2025 Report (with Lighthouse Research)
smartrecruiters_benchmarks_2025.methodology: Nearly 90 million applications for 1.5 million jobs across 95 countries
key_findings: Global median time to hire is 38 days; US is 35 days (8% faster)
73 applications per opening globally but only 3 interviewed, 1 hired
Companies using AI hire 26% faster, saving approximately 11 days
US recruiters handle 54 hires/month, 85% more than global average of 30
Healthcare leads internal mobility at 20% of hires; tech leads referrals at 16%

indeed_hiring_lab_2025.title: 2025 US Jobs & Hiring Trends Report
key_findings: Job postings fell to 2017 levels by end of 2025 despite population growth
1 million more unemployed than available jobs by Dec 2025, widest gap since 2017
Healthcare accounts for 65% of jobs added in 2025; tech remains one of weakest sectors
Pay transparency rose to 57.8% of postings; degree requirements fell to 17.6%
Low-hire, low-fire environment persisted through 2025 ('Great Stay')

linkedin_future_of_recruiting_2025.title: The Future of Recruiting 2025
linkedin_future_of_recruiting_2025.methodology: Billions of data points from LinkedIn platform, survey of 1,000+ talent professionals, advice from dozens of talent leaders

key_metrics.recruiter_skill_relationship_development: 54x more likely to be listed as required

key_findings: 75% of recruiters say skills-based hiring will be their top priority
61% of TA pros believe AI can improve quality of hire measurement
Companies using AI-Assisted Messaging are 9% more likely to make quality hires
25% of LinkedIn job postings already omit degree requirements
Internal mobility increased 6% year-over-year

glassdoor_worklife_trends_2025.title: Glassdoor Worklife Trends 2025 & 2026
key_findings: 65% of professionals feel stuck in current roles
Employee-leadership disconnect mentions rose 24%, misalignment surged 149%
Gen Z makes up ~20% of workforce; 1 in 10 managers in 2025 will be Gen Z
Layoff mentions in reviews higher in late 2025 than March 2020 levels
Remote/hybrid worker career opportunity ratings fell from 4.1 to 3.5 (2020-2025)

ziprecruiter_labor_market_2025.title: 2025 Annual Employer Survey & New Hires Survey
ziprecruiter_labor_market_2025.methodology: Survey of 1,500 businesses of varying sizes and industries throughout the U.S.
key_findings: Turnover plummeted from 177% (2023) to 49.5% (2025) - 'The Great Freeze'
63% of businesses plan to increase hiring, especially entry-level roles
27.3% of new hires accepted pay cuts in Q4 2025, up from 24.5% in Q3
Only 15.4% received signing bonuses in Q4, down from 21.7% in Q3
Top 3 skills employers seek are all soft skills: time management, attention to detail, critical thinking

handshake_early_talent_2025.title: 2025 Campus to Career Report & Gen Z Hiring Trends
key_findings: Median full-time starting salary rose to $67,500 in 2025 (+$2,500 YoY)
Pay transparency in postings up to 77% full-time, 81% internship
Remote job availability stabilized at ~5%, down from 7% peak in 2022
Gen Z will make up a third of the workforce by 2030
Class of 2025 hiring expected to increase 7.3% over Class of 2024 (NACE data)

shrm_benchmarking_2025.title: 2025 Recruiting Executives Benchmarking Report
shrm_benchmarking_2025.methodology: 2,371 SHRM members surveyed Jan 9 - Mar 3, 2025, with stratified sampling of VP+ executives
key_findings: Median time to fill is 45 days for both executive and non-executive roles
Non-executive cost per hire: $1,200 median; Executive: $10,625 (7x more expensive)
Executive hiring costs up 113% since 2017 and 21% since 2022
Only 20% of organizations track quality of hire in a meaningful way
AI adoption for HR tasks jumped 65% in one year (26% to 43%); enterprise at 78%

gartner_ta_trends_2025_2026.title: Gartner HR Research: Talent Acquisition Trends 2025-2026
four_ta_trends_2026: Reimagine recruiting model with AI-powered innovation
Harness data-driven insights for talent strategy agility
Evolve assessment practices to safeguard talent quality amid candidate fraud
Align attraction and sourcing strategies with shifting talent demands
key_findings: 72% of recruiting leaders say HR needs to be more agile than pre-pandemic
25% of workers will need updated skills (up from 8% in 2020), driven by GenAI
By 2027, 75% of hiring will include certifications and tests for AI proficiency
1 in 5 employees will need to be redeployed by 2030
HR will turn one-third of its recruiting capacity inward for internal mobility

josh_bersin_ta_revolution_2025.title: The Talent Acquisition Revolution: How AI Is Transforming Recruiting
four_focus_areas: Implementing skills-based approaches to hiring and mobility
Redesigning TA tech stack with AI at the forefront
Developing multifaceted approach to fill talent and skills gaps
Upskilling and elevating the TA function for the future
key_findings: TA market valued at $850 billion, growing at 13% per year
Only 17% of applicants reach interview stage; 60% abandon slow/complex applications
AI-enabled technology yields 2-3x faster time to hire
Only 20% of companies use skills insights for hiring; 9% have skills-based marketplace
TA is evolving from manual, reactive function into strategic, AI-powered ecosystem

lighthouse_ta_trends_2025.title: 2025 Talent Acquisition Trends Study
lighthouse_ta_trends_2025.methodology: Survey of 1,000 global candidates and 1,013 TA/HR leaders
key_findings: 84% of employers embracing or exploring skills-first hiring strategy
Assessments are now the top tool for managing high applicant volumes (2x video interviews)
Candidate #1 priority is smooth/fast application process; employers rank it only #3
Proactive TA teams are 2x more likely to use AI saving recruiters 5+ hours weekly
AI reshaping entry-level/administrative roles across retail, logistics, finance, customer service

aptitude_research_2025.title: Aptitude Research: Talent Acquisition & Programmatic Advertising 2025
key_findings: 84% of CHROs say talent intelligence is a top priority
Average company invests in 10+ tech solutions for recruitment; 32% consolidating
52% of TA leaders say office mandates hinder recruitment; 72% find remote easier
73% rank critical thinking as #1 recruiting priority; AI skills rank 5th
65% of candidates lose interest after a bad interview experience

sia_staffing_market_2025.title: Global Staffing Market Estimates & Forecasts 2025-2030
key_findings: US staffing market forecast to grow 1% in 2025 to $188.7B after 10% decline in 2024
Global staffing revenue was $626B in 2024; US generates ~25% of global revenue
Temporary staffing hours still down 4% YoY in March 2025; professional staffing down 7%
Asia Pacific strongest performing region, with India (13%) and China (16%) growth
26,000 staffing firms in US operating ~49,000 offices

korn_ferry_talent_shortage_2025.title: Future of Work: The Global Talent Crunch & Workforce 2025
korn_ferry_talent_shortage_2025.methodology: Workforce 2025 survey: 15,000 global employees across job roles, age demographics, and industries. TA Trends: 400 talent professionals across 13 industries.
key_findings: By 2030: 85 million person talent shortage could result in $8.5T in unrealized revenue
US could lose $162B annually in tech revenue alone due to talent shortage
67% of talent pros see AI having major role; 40% worry about losing human touch
64% of TA leaders find it challenging to find candidates willing to work in office
67% of employees would stay if offered advancement/upskilling, even if they hated their job

manpowergroup_talent_shortage_2025.title: 2025 Global Talent Shortage Survey
key_findings: 74% of employers globally report difficulty finding skilled talent (down from 77% in 2024)
Germany (86%), Israel (85%), and Portugal (84%) face the worst shortages
Health/Medical/Life Sciences (84%) and IT (83%) industries face greatest shortages
Shortage more than doubled in a decade: from 36% (2014) to 74% (2025)
First decrease in 10 years suggests slight easing, but levels remain historically high

robert_half_salary_guide_2025.title: 2025 Salary Guide
robert_half_salary_guide_2025.methodology: Online survey of 2,500+ hiring managers across finance/accounting, technology, marketing/creative, legal, admin, and HR at companies with 20+ employees
key_findings: Salaries increased 3.0% YoY across 7 professions tracked
66% of managers willing to pay more for in-office work; 59% up to 20% premium
54% say AI/automation reshaping needed skill sets; 37% hiring contractors for AI projects
48% rank meeting salary expectations as top hiring challenge
Finance & accounting saw most robust compensation growth amid regulatory demand

gem_recruiting_benchmarks_2025.title: 2025 Recruiting Benchmarks Report (5th Annual)
gem_recruiting_benchmarks_2025.methodology: Data from 140+ million applications, 14 million candidates, and 1 million hires
key_findings: Average time to hire increased 24% to 41 days (from 33 in 2021)
Interviews per hire up 42% to 20 (from 14 in 2021)
Offer acceptance rate improved to 84% (from 81% in 2021, Great Resignation era)
Recruiter teams shrank from 31 to 24 while managing 56% more reqs and 2.7x more applications
Sourced candidates 5x more likely to be hired than inbound; talent rediscovery rose to 44%

avg_cost_per_hire_by_industry.source: SHRM 2025, Radancy, industry aggregations from multiple reports

healthcare_life_sciences.note: Highly variable; specialist roles significantly higher

shrm_median_nonexecutive.note: SHRM median; represents direct recruitment costs only

appcast_recruitment_ad_cph.note: Recruitment advertising cost per hire only (not total CPH)

avg_time_to_fill_by_industry.source: SHRM 2025, SmartRecruiters 2025, Gem 2025, multiple industry reports

appcast_overall_trend.note: Down substantially from 2023 across all occupations

cpc_trends.note: CPCs rose 27% in 2024 driven by harder-to-fill roles and job board pricing

it_technology.note: High applicant volume but talent mismatch

workforce_mobility_and_turnover.era_label: The Great Stay / The Great Freeze

top_hiring_trends_2025.description: Record-low quits rates, plummeting turnover, and reduced mobility define the 2025 labor market. Workers stay put due to economic uncertainty.
data_points: Quits rate at 1.9% (lowest since 2015)
Turnover dropped from 177% to 49.5%
First-year turnover fell 49% to 12.1%

top_hiring_trends_2025.description: AI adoption in HR surged 65% YoY but 83% of organizations remain at low maturity. AI reduces time-to-hire by 26% and yields 2-3x faster results.
data_points: 43% of organizations use AI for HR (up from 26%)
78% of enterprises use AI in recruitment
Only 5% at high automation maturity

top_hiring_trends_2025.description: 84% of employers embracing skills-first strategies. Degree requirements dropped to 17.6% of postings. Critical thinking ranked #1 priority over AI skills.
data_points: 75% of recruiters say skills-based hiring is top priority
25% of LinkedIn postings omit degree requirements
73% of TA leaders rank critical thinking as #1

top_hiring_trends_2025.description: Dramatic occupational divide: tech/office roles flooded with applicants while healthcare/trades face critical shortages.
data_points: Computer/math roles: 65 APO vs Healthcare practitioners: 15 APO
Technology apply rate: 6.41% vs Healthcare: much lower
Healthcare accounts for 65% of 2025 job growth

top_hiring_trends_2025.trend: Rising Recruitment Costs Despite Softer Market
top_hiring_trends_2025.description: CPC rose 27%, CPA and CPH rose sharply in 2025 due to job board pricing changes and programmatic model shifts.
data_points: CPC up 27% in 2024
CPA and CPH rose sharply in 2025
Executive CPH up 113% since 2017 to $10,625 median

top_hiring_trends_2025.trend: Recruiter Teams Doing More With Less
top_hiring_trends_2025.description: Teams shrank 23% (31 to 24) while managing 56% more reqs and 2.7x more applications. Efficiency gains through AI and automation.
data_points: 14 open reqs per recruiter (up 56%)
2,500+ applications per recruiter (up 2.7x)
US recruiters handle 54 hires/month vs 30 global avg

top_hiring_trends_2025.description: 60% of candidates abandon slow applications. 25% turn down jobs due to poor experience. Smooth/fast process is candidate #1 priority.
data_points: Only 17% of applicants reach interview stage
65% lose interest after bad interview
Offer acceptance rate improved to 84%

top_hiring_trends_2025.description: Pay transparency in postings reached 57.8%. Salary growth at 3.0-3.5%. 27% of new hires accepting pay cuts by Q4 2025.
data_points: 57.8% of postings include pay info (up from 52.2%)
Median entry-level salary: $67,500
66% of managers willing to pay 20% premium for in-office work

top_hiring_trends_2025.description: 74% of employers globally can't find skilled talent. By 2030: 85M person shortage, $8.5T unrealized revenue.
data_points: Shortage doubled in a decade: 36% (2014) to 74% (2025)
Healthcare/Life Sciences: 84% shortage
IT industry: 83% shortage

top_hiring_trends_2025.description: Organizations shifting 33% of recruiting capacity inward. Internal mobility up 6% YoY. Healthcare leads at 20% internal hires.
data_points: 67% of employees would stay for advancement opportunities
Healthcare: 20% internal hires
76% of employers focus on retention
